description_long: This object is a building with a prominent pitched roof, featuring a large array of blue solar panels installed on one side of the roof. The main structure is rectangular with a pinkish exterior, and an attached flat-roofed section extends from one side, possibly serving as an auxiliary area or equipment housing. The solar panels are arranged in a stepped, multi-row configuration, covering a significant portion of the sloped roof. The roof itself is covered in reddish-brown tiles, and a small rectangular skylight or vent is visible at the roof's peak. The side of the building displays the name 'Raltec Energy,' indicating its association with solar energy infrastructure.
description: A pink building with a pitched, reddish-brown tiled roof, featuring a large stepped array of blue solar panels and a labeled side reading 'Raltec Energy.'
description_view_0: The main pitched roof with reddish-brown tiles and a small skylight is visible, along with the attached flat-roofed section.
description_view_1: The sloped roof with the blue solar panels is prominent, and the flat-roofed extension is clearly seen on the right.
description_view_2: The stepped array of blue solar panels dominates the roof, and the 'Raltec Energy' label is visible on the building's side.
description_view_3: The solar panels are seen from above, showing their stepped arrangement, and the flat-roofed section is at the rear.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing outward from the main roof slope, fixed orientation
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted, likely supported by an aluminum frame integrated into the tiled roof
attribute_array_configuration: multi-row stepped array, approximately 3 rows of 3-4 panels each, covering one side of the pitched roof
